alt: Wide view of snow-covered glaciated volcanic peaks of Deception Island in the South Shetland Islands of Antarctica, with the abandoned Whalers Bay whaling-station structures — dormitory shed and rust-red oil storage tanks — visible along the black volcanic shoreline beneath low fog
description: Wide view of Deception Island in the South Shetland Islands, Antarctica — the snow-covered glaciated volcanic caldera under low fog, with the abandoned whaling-station structures of Whalers Bay (dormitory shed and rust-red oil storage tanks) visible along the black volcanic shoreline. The flooded caldera is the most-visited site in the South Shetland Islands; vessels enter through Neptune's Bellows on the eastern side.
title: Deception Island
